paper_overview.citation: Biswas, P., & Vellanki, B. P. (2021). Occurrence of emerging contaminants in highly anthropogenically influenced river Yamuna in India. Science of the Total Environment, 782, 146741.
paper_overview.study_region: River Yamuna in Northern India, including its tributaries Hindon river and Hindon canal. Specific locations include Dakpatthar, Delhi (Nizamuddin bridge, Okhla barrage, D/S Shahadara drain & Yamuna Confluence), Mathura (U/S and D/S), Agra (U/S, near Taj Mahal, D/S), and Etawah (U/S and D/S).
temporal_coverage.sampling_dates: Summer sampling was conducted in May, and post-monsoon sampling was conducted in September.
extraction_notes: CAS numbers and supplier information for the standards used in this study are provided in supplementary information (S-1). The method for solid phase extraction is detailed in supplementary information (S-5). The methods used for HPLC-MS and UPLC-QToF analysis, along with Method Detection Limits (MDL) and Method Quantification Limits (MQL), are given in supplementary information (S-6).
site.description: Dakpatthar, located in the lower Himalayas, the most upstream sampling point with a sparse population, close to the origin of Yamuna.
site.source_quote: The first sampling point Dakpatthar (Y-1) is situated close to the origin of Yamuna at the lower Himalayas with a sparse population.
